title: hieracium murorum subsp. nastophyllum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. nastophyllum, also known as Wall Hawkweed or Nastophyllum, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Wall Hawkweed has been associated with several health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, Wall Hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its bitter flavor is often balanced by mixing it with other herbs.
